title: Panel Structured Output
description: Required structured emission appended to every panel review. Panels continue emitting Markdown reasoning but must append this JSON block. Execution fails if the structured block is missing.

panel_name.description: Identifier matching the panel filename without extension (e.g., 'code-review', 'security-review').

panel_version.description: Semantic version of the panel definition used for this execution.

confidence_score.description: Aggregate confidence in the panel's assessment. 0.0 = no confidence, 1.0 = full confidence.

risk_level.description: Assessed risk level for the reviewed change.

compliance_score.description: Degree of compliance with applicable policies. 0.0 = non-compliant, 1.0 = fully compliant.

policy_flags.description: Policy violation or concern flags raised during review.

auto_remediable.description: Whether this flag can be resolved without human intervention.

requires_human_review.description: Whether the panel recommends human review before merge.

findings.description: Individual findings from each persona in the panel.

confidence.description: This persona's confidence in their verdict.

rationale.description: Brief rationale for the verdict. Must not be empty.

evidence.description: Grounding evidence linking this finding to a specific code location. Findings with severity 'medium' or above that lack evidence may be treated as hallucinated and discarded.

file.description: File path where the finding was identified

groundedness_score.description: Score indicating how well this finding is grounded in actual code evidence. 0.0 = no grounding, 1.0 = fully grounded with concrete evidence.

hallucination_indicators.description: Indicators suggesting potential hallucination in this finding. Empty array means no hallucination signals detected.

ai_act_risk_tier.description: EU AI Act risk classification tier for the assessed change.

destruction_recommended.description: Whether the panel recommends resource destruction or shutdown. When true, requires_human_approval must also be true. Used by FinOps review to flag decommission candidates.

requires_human_approval.description: Whether the change requires explicit human approval before proceeding. Set to true when destruction_recommended is true or when the panel identifies actions that must not be automated.

execution_status.description: Status of the panel execution. Defaults to 'success' if omitted (backward compatible).

aggregate_verdict.description: Computed aggregate verdict based on individual persona verdicts and policy weighting.

execution_context.description: Contextual metadata about the panel execution environment.

model_id.description: Canonical model identifier from the model registry (e.g., 'claude-opus-4-6', 'gpt-4-turbo'). Preferred over model_version for provenance tracking.

model_version.description: Model version string for fine-grained version details (e.g., training date suffixes, deployment variants). Retained for backward compatibility.

model_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the model weights or version identifier.

version_date.description: Model version or training cutoff date.

system_prompt_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the system prompt used for this panel execution. Enables detection of prompt drift.

inference_config.description: Inference parameters used for this panel execution.

token_count.description: Token usage for this panel execution. Enables cost tracking and budget enforcement.

estimated_cost_usd.description: Estimated cost in USD for this panel execution, derived from token_count and backend pricing.

data_classification.description: Required classification of the emission's data sensitivity. All panels must classify data they reference and apply redaction rules for sensitive content.

level.description: Data sensitivity level of this emission's content. Must reflect the highest classification of any data referenced in findings.

contains_sensitive_evidence.description: Whether findings contain sensitive evidence (credentials, PII, vulnerability details). Must be true if any finding references secrets, PII, or exploit details.

redaction_applied.description: Whether sensitive content has been redacted from this emission. Must be true when contains_sensitive_evidence is true.

sensitive_categories.description: Categories of sensitive data detected in the reviewed change. Empty array if no sensitive data was found.

redaction_rules_applied.description: List of redaction rules that were applied to sanitize this emission.

schema_version.description: Schema version this emission conforms to. Defaults to 1.0.0 if omitted.

canary_results.description: Results from canary calibration tests injected during panel execution. Optional — omit if no canaries were injected.

canary_id.description: Identifier of the canary test (matches canary-calibration.yaml).

detected.description: Whether the panel detected the known issue in the canary snippet.

actual_severity.description: Severity reported by the panel, or null if not detected.

severity_match.description: Whether the reported severity matches the expected severity.

detection_latency_ms.description: Time taken to detect the canary finding, if measurable.

execution_trace.description: Evidence of actual code evaluation during panel review. Records which files the LLM read and which specific code sections it analyzed, linking findings to concrete code locations.

files_read.description: File paths the panel agent read during review.

analysis_duration_ms.description: Approximate time spent on analysis in milliseconds.

grounding_references.description: Links findings to specific code locations.
